title: Build a Narrow-Screen Baseline before Enhancements
summary: Build and verify responsive mobile first in resource inventory narrow-layout baseline through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain responsive mobile first from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply responsive mobile first without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

options.text: Resource inventory narrow-layout baseline case 1: mobile first means targeting one standard phone width and adding desktop overrides later.
Resource inventory narrow-layout baseline case 1: make the unqueried base complete at constrained widths, then layer enhancements from observed content needs without removing tasks.
Resource inventory narrow-layout baseline case 1: hide secondary table columns on phones because narrow layouts need less information.

steps.title: 04 · Read the resource inventory narrow-layout baseline case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim preserves responsive mobile first when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: resource inventory narrow-layout baseline.
steps.why: Build a Narrow-Screen Baseline before Enhancements makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in resource inventory narrow-layout baseline.

content.text: Mobile-first CSS begins with a complete content-driven base that works at the narrowest supported inline size, with zoom, long labels, keyboard focus, and coarse input. Enhancements come later when available space supports them. It is an ordering strategy for resilient rules, not a design for one phone model or a reason to hide content. Apply it to resource inventory narrow-layout baseline.
